title: File System Implementation Architecture
description: A flowchart illustrating the layered components and their interactions in a typical file system implementation, from the user application interface down to the physical disk blocks.
elements: User Application (API Calls: open, read, write, close)
Logical File System (Manages metadata & directory structure)
File Organization Module (Translates logical blocks to physical blocks)
Basic File System (Issues generic read/write commands to device drivers)
relationships: User requests flow downward through the layers
Each layer uses the services of the layer below it
Data from the disk flows upward through the layers to the user
